violations.text: The floor in areas of the physical facilities was observed with a buildup of food and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and droppings... - The flattop grill area was observed to have a heavy accumulation of debris and grease behind it on the electrical and gas lines. Per the PIC, a professional cleaning company is to be called back to thoroughly clean the area. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and droppings... - The flattop grill area was observed to have a heavy accumulation of debris and grease below it. Per the PIC, a professional cleaning company is due to thoroughly clean the area. - The floor under/behind the large 2 door reach-in beverage cooler was observed to have an accumulation of dust and droppings. Per the PIC, the beverage company is due to move the unit so thorough cleaning can be completed. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

A dead mouse was observed on a glue trap in the utility closet in the women's restroom. Dead or trapped rodents and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. (Dead Mouse was removed at the time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings observed on the floor throughout the facility, under and behind equipment, and on non-food-contact equipment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per the PIC, the current pest control program is set treatment as called/needed. Pest control program is to be increased to twice weekly until controlled. (Pest Control arrived at the time of inspection.) New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

At the time of inspection, clean utensils and backup utensils were observed stored in containers with debris and pooling water. Utensils are to be stored in clean equipment to avoid possible contamination during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are not stored inverted to allow for proper air drying. Once equipment is washed and sanitized, equipment is to be stored inverted to allow for proper air drying. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The non-food-contact surfaces of the in the food service and kitchen areas were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues and to aid in the pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The floor in the front service area, the food prep/grill area, and the back prep/storage areas was observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025
Several ceiling tiles were observed to be cracked/chipped creating gaps and exposing the structure area above. Ceiling is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The tile grout in the back kitchen area is in poor repair. Grout lines were observed to be worn/chipped/deteriorated. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2023

Meatballs, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 109 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Meatballs were microwaved to above 165. Per area manager, hot hold unit should be set to 10 to reheat food above 165. Hot hold unit was only set to 7. Establishment to monitor reheating temperatures to ensure food is properly reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2023

The presence of flying bugs is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of flying bugs near the mop area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023

The grout in the ware-washing/ mop area is in poor repair. The grout was worn down allowing for pooling water and excess food debris. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2023
